Aya Ahmed is a Python Developer with contact information including email (aya.ahmed@email.com), phone number (+20 101 234 5678), GitHub profile (github.com/aya-ahmed), and LinkedIn profile (linkedin.com/in/aya-ahmed).
Aya Ahmed received a Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) from Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Marathwada University in Aurangabad, India.

Aya Ahmed worked as a Python Developer at Arsys Inovics Pvt Ltd in Cairo, Egypt, participating in all phases of project development, including design, development, deployment, testing, implementation, and support.

During her time at Arsys Inovics, Aya Ahmed developed views and templates using Python and Django’s MVT architecture, created CRUD methods for API interaction, contributed to both back-end and front-end development, wrote Python routines for web crawling, implemented features like navigation and pagination, created unit tests, utilized Agile processes and Jira, and managed code with Git and GitHub.

Aya Ahmed developed the F-MAS (Frequency Monitoring and Analysis System) for telecommunications service providers, including an Operations Support System (OSS) component supporting Public Switched Telephone Networks. The system utilized Python, Django, MySQL, HTML, CSS, SQLAlchemy, JSON, REST Web Services, and Urllib.
Aya Ahmed possesses skills in Python, Django, Django REST Framework (DRF), MySQL, Oracle, SQLite, MongoDB, HTML, CSS, RESTful Web Services, JSON, Git, GitHub, Agile, Scrum, Jira, Windows, and Unix.

Aya Ahmed is a resourceful and technically proficient Python Developer, forged in the crucible of both Indian education and Egyptian industry. Holding a Bachelor of Engineering from Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Marathwada University, she quickly established herself at Arsys Inovics Pvt Ltd in Cairo, diving into all stages of the software development lifecycle – from initial design and meticulous testing to seamless deployment and ongoing support. 

Aya isn’t simply a coder; she’s a builder. Proficient in Python and the Django framework, she crafts elegant views and templates using the MVT architecture, and expertly designs CRUD methods for robust API interaction. Equally comfortable on the front-end and back-end, she’s a full-stack contributor, capable of weaving together compelling user experiences. Her skillset extends to web crawling with Python routines, implementing crucial features like navigation and pagination, and ensuring code quality through diligent unit testing. 

Her most significant achievement to date is the development of F-MAS (Frequency Monitoring and Analysis System), a sophisticated solution for telecommunications providers, complete with an Operations Support System (OSS) component for Public Switched Telephone Networks. This project showcases her mastery of a diverse technology stack including Django, MySQL, HTML, CSS, SQLAlchemy, JSON, REST Web Services, and Urllib. 

Aya operates with precision and collaboration, embracing Agile methodologies and utilizing Jira for project management. She’s a committed version control advocate, expertly managing code with Git and GitHub. Beyond core languages, she’s fluent in a range of databases – MySQL, Oracle, SQLite, and MongoDB – and comfortable navigating both Windows and Unix environments. You can find her work and connect with her through GitHub (github.com/aya-ahmed) and LinkedIn (linkedin.com/in/aya-ahmed), or reach her directly at aya.ahmed@email.com or +20 101 234 5678.

Django is a high-level Python web framework, a powerful engine for crafting robust and scalable web applications. Built around the Model-View-Template (MVT) architectural pattern, it provides a clean and pragmatic design, encouraging rapid development and clean, maintainable code. More than just a toolkit, Django is a complete solution, handling everything from URL routing and HTTP management to security and database interaction. 

It’s a favorite amongst developers like Aya Ahmed, who leverages its capabilities to build complex systems like the F-MAS (Frequency Monitoring and Analysis System) – a sophisticated platform for telecommunications providers. Within F-MAS, Django orchestrates the interaction between databases like MySQL and the user interface, delivering data through RESTful web services and JSON formats. 

Django isn’t limited to back-end tasks; it’s equally adept at powering dynamic front-ends, handling features like navigation, pagination, and API interactions with ease. Its versatility extends to web crawling through custom Python routines, and a strong emphasis on testing ensures reliability.  A skilled hand with Django can build anything from simple websites to complex, data-driven applications, managing the entire process with tools like Git and GitHub, and within the collaborative framework of Agile methodologies. It’s a framework built for both power and practicality, a cornerstone of modern web development.
